description_long: This is a wooden picket fence section composed of vertical planks with visible gaps between each slat, giving it a semi-open appearance. The planks are weathered and painted in a muted green, suggesting outdoor use and exposure to the elements. Two horizontal rails run across the back, securing the vertical slats in place. The top edge of the fence is uneven, with alternating plank heights, creating a slightly irregular silhouette. There are no decorative elements or post caps, and the construction appears utilitarian and straightforward. This type of fence is commonly found in urban yards, parks, or as a boundary along sidewalks.
description: A weathered green wooden picket fence with vertical slats of varying heights and two horizontal rails, featuring a simple, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the vertical green planks with visible gaps and the irregular top edge.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the alignment of the planks.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the two horizontal rails that secure the vertical slats.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the staggered plank heights.
